𝗩𝗜𝗘𝗪𝗘𝗥𝗠𝗘𝗧𝗥𝗜𝗖𝗦 | Analyzing live stream viewership to identify and filter out bot accounts, providing streamers with accurate audience metrics.  🔥 Super Hot!

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Analyzing live stream viewership to identify and filter out bot accounts, providing streamers with accurate audience metrics.
📅 Created: Oct 2025 • Updated: May 2026

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Track viewer counts and authenticated users
✅ Detect potential bot accounts
✅ View detailed user profiles
✅ Identify suspicious account creation patterns
✅ Automatic adaptation to channels
✅ Visual charts of legitimate vs. bot distribution
✅ Searchable viewer list
✅ Auto-adjusts request rates


┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 170 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 13 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 2 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

The Viewer Metrics Chrome Extension tracks and analyzes live stream viewers, differentiating between genuine users and potential bot accounts. It provides detailed analytics, user profiles, and visual charts to help streamers understand their audience composition.

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/viewermetrics/viewermetrics

𝗗𝗢𝗖𝗞-𝗗𝗥𝗢𝗜𝗗 | Running and testing Android environments within Docker containers for development, security research, and CI/CD pipelines.  🔥 Super Hot!

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📊 Dockerfile 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Running and testing Android environments within Docker containers for development, security research, and CI/CD pipelines.
📅 Created: Jul 2021 • Updated: May 2026

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Run QEMU Android x86 and ARM in Docker
✅ X11 Forwarding
✅ ADB on port :5555
✅ Magisk, riru, LSPosed support on Android x86
✅ SSH enabled
✅ SCRCPY enabled
✅ WebCam forwarding
✅ Audio forwarding
✅ GPU passthrough
✅ Support for BlissOS, custom images, VDI files, any Android x86 image, Xvfb headless mode
✅ Container cloning with `docker commit`


❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.4K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 109 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 22 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Dock-Droid allows users to run Android x86 and ARM environments within Docker containers using QEMU and KVM. It facilitates advanced use cases like security research, CI/CD pipelines, and app testing with features like X11 forwarding, ADB access, and GPU passthrough.

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/sickcodes/dock-droid

𝗦𝗢𝗖𝗞𝗧𝗢𝗣 | Remote system monitoring with a user-friendly TUI.  🔥 Super Hot!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Remote system monitoring with a user-friendly TUI.
📅 Created: Aug 2025 • Updated: May 2026

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Remote monitoring via WebSocket (JSON over WS)
✅ Optional WSS (TLS) with client certificate pinning
✅ TUI built with ratatui
✅ Detailed CPU, Memory, Swap, Disk, Network, and Temperature metrics
✅ Sortable process table with scrollbars
✅ Optional GPU metrics
✅ Optional agent authentication token


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 157 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 9 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Socktop is a remote system monitoring tool that provides a rich TUI interface, inspired by `top` and `btop`. It communicates with a lightweight agent over WebSockets, offering detailed system metrics and process information.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/jasonwitty/socktop

𝗖𝗥𝗢𝗡𝗜𝗖𝗟𝗘 | A distributed task scheduler and runner with a web-based UI, serving as an advanced replacement for traditional cron jobs across multiple servers.  🔥 Super Hot!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: A distributed task scheduler and runner with a web-based UI, serving as an advanced replacement for traditional cron jobs across multiple servers.
📅 Created: Jan 2016 • Updated: May 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Single or multi-server setup
✅ Automated failover to backup servers
✅ Auto-discovery of nearby servers
✅ Real-time job status with live log viewer
✅ Plugins can be written in any language
✅ Schedule events in multiple timezones
✅ Track CPU and memory usage for each job
✅ Historical stats with performance graphs
✅ Simple REST API for scheduling and running events


⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Cronicle is a robust, multi-server task scheduler and runner written in Node.js, offering a web-based UI for managing scheduled and on-demand jobs. It provides features like automated failover, real-time job status, live log viewing, and the ability to write plugins in any language.

𝗪𝗘𝗕-𝗟𝗟𝗠 | To enable high-performance, privacy-preserving LLM inference directly within web browsers, leveraging local hardware acceleration (WebGPU) and offering OpenAI API compatibility for a wide range of open-source models.  🔥 Super Hot!

📅 Created: Apr 2023 • Updated: May 2026

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

WebLLM is a high-performance inference engine that runs Large Language Models (LLMs) directly in web browsers using WebGPU for hardware acceleration. It offers full compatibility with the OpenAI API, enabling local execution of various open-source models with features like streaming and JSON mode, all without requiring server support.
